name: Encrypt Secrets with Ansible Vault
description: Encrypt sensitive variables and files using Ansible Vault to safely store passwords, API keys, and certificates in version control.

steps.title: Create an encrypted variables file
steps.description: Opens an editor to create a new encrypted file. You will be prompted for a vault password.
steps.tip: Use a naming convention like vault.yml to clearly mark encrypted files.

steps.title: Encrypt an existing file
steps.description: Encrypts a plain-text file in place. The original content is replaced with encrypted data.

steps.title: View encrypted file contents
steps.description: Displays the decrypted content without modifying the file.

steps.title: Edit an encrypted file
steps.description: Decrypts the file into a temporary buffer, opens your editor, and re-encrypts on save.

steps.title: Run a playbook with vault-encrypted files
steps.description: Prompts for the vault password at runtime to decrypt any vault-encrypted files.
steps.tip: Use --vault-password-file to point to a file or script that provides the password for non-interactive use in CI.

fullScript:
# Ansible Vault secrets management
ansible-vault create group_vars/production/vault.yml
ansible-vault encrypt group_vars/production/secrets.yml
ansible-vault view group_vars/production/vault.yml
ansible-vault edit group_vars/production/vault.yml
ansible-playbook site.yml --ask-vault-pass

faq.question: Can I use different vault passwords for different environments?
faq.answer: Yes. Use vault IDs: 'ansible-vault encrypt --vault-id prod@prompt secrets.yml' and 'ansible-playbook site.yml --vault-id prod@prompt --vault-id dev@prompt' to handle multiple passwords.

faq.question: How do I encrypt a single variable instead of a whole file?
faq.answer: Use 'ansible-vault encrypt_string "my_secret_value" --name "db_password"'. Paste the output into your vars file. Only that variable is encrypted while the rest remains readable.

faq.question: Is Ansible Vault encryption secure?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses AES-256 symmetric encryption. The vault password is the only key, so use a strong password and manage it securely.
